en: Now when the Pharisees gathered to him, with some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem,
zh: 有法利賽人和幾個經學家從耶路撒冷來，聚集到耶穌那裡。

en: they saw that some of his disciples ate with hands that were defiled, that is, unwashed.
zh: 他們看見他的門徒有人用不潔的手，就是沒有洗過的手吃飯，

en: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands, holding to the tradition of the elders,

and when they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other traditions that they observe, such as the washing of cups and pots and copper vessels and dining couches.)
And the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, "Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?"
zh: 法利賽人和經學家問耶穌：“你的門徒為甚麼不遵行古人的傳統，用不潔的手吃飯呢？”

en: And he said to them, "Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written, "' This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me;
zh: 耶穌對他們說：“以賽亞指著你們這班偽君子所說的預言是對的，經上記著： ‘這人民用嘴唇尊敬我， 心卻遠離我；

en: in vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.'
zh: 他們把人的規條當作道理去教導人， 所以拜我也是徒然。’

en: You leave the commandment of God and hold to the tradition of men."
zh: 你們拘守著人的傳統，卻離棄了 神的誡命。”

en: And he said to them, "You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to establish your tradition!
zh: 耶穌又對他們說：“你們為了堅守自己的傳統，而巧妙地把 神的誡命拒絕了。

en: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother'; and, 'Whoever reviles father or mother must surely die.'
zh: 因為摩西說：‘當孝敬父母’，又說：‘咒罵父母的，必被處死。’

en: But you say, 'If a man tells his father or his mother, Whatever you would have gained from me is Corban' (that is, given to God)-
zh: 你們倒說：‘人對父母說，我應該給你的供奉，已經作了各耳板’ ，

en: then you no longer permit him to do anything for his father or mother,
zh: 你們就不讓那人再為父母作甚麼。

en: thus making void the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And many such things you do."
zh: 這樣，你們藉著所領受的傳統，把 神的話廢棄了。你們還作了許多這一類的事。”

en: And he called the people to him again and said to them, "Hear me, all of you, and understand:
zh: 於是耶穌又把群眾叫過來，對他們說：“你們大家都要聽我說，也要明白：

en: There is nothing outside a person that by going into him can defile him, but the things that come out of a person are what defile him."
zh: 從外面進去的，不能使人污穢，從裡面出來的，才能使人污穢。”

en: And when he had entered the house and left the people, his disciples asked him about the parable.
zh: 耶穌離開群眾，進了屋子，門徒就來問他這比喻的意思。

en: And he said to them, "Then are you also without understanding? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him,
zh: 他對他們說：“連你們也是這樣不明白嗎？難道不知道從外面進去的，不能使人污穢嗎？

en: since it enters not his heart but his stomach, and is expelled?" (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
zh: 因為不是進到他的心，而是進到他的肚腹，再排泄到外面 去。”

en: And he said, "What comes out of a person is what defiles him.
zh: 接著他又說：“從人裡面出來的，才會使人污穢。

en: For from within, out of the heart of man, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,
zh: 因為從裡面，就是從人的心裡，發出惡念、淫亂、偷盜、兇殺、

en: coveting, wickedness, deceit, sensuality, envy, slander, pride, foolishness.
zh: 姦淫、貪心、邪惡、詭詐、放蕩、嫉妒、毀謗、驕傲、愚妄；

en: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person."
zh: 這一切惡事，是從人裡面出來的，都能使人污穢。”

en: And from there he arose and went away to the region of Tyre and Sidon. And he entered a house and did not want anyone to know, yet he could not be hidden.
zh: 耶穌從那裡動身到推羅 境內去。進了一所房子，本來不想讓人知道，卻隱藏不住。

en: But immediately a woman whose little daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard of him and came and fell down at his feet.
zh: 有一個女人，她的小女兒被污靈附著，她聽見了耶穌的事，就來俯伏在他腳前。

en: Now the woman was a Gentile, a Syrophoenician by birth. And she begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
zh: 這女人是外族人，屬於敘利亞的腓尼基族。她求耶穌把鬼從她女兒身上趕出去。

en: And he said to her, "Let the children be fed first, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs."
zh: 耶穌對她說：“應該先讓兒女吃飽。拿兒女的餅去丟給小狗吃是不好的。”

en: But she answered him, "Yes, Lord; yet even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."
zh: 那女人回答他：“主啊，是的，不過小狗在桌子底下，也可以吃孩子們掉下來的碎渣。”

en: And he said to her, "For this statement you may go your way; the demon has left your daughter."
zh: 耶穌對她說：“就憑這句話，你回去吧，鬼已經從你女兒身上出去了。”

en: And she went home and found the child lying in bed and the demon gone.
zh: 她回到家裡，看見小孩子躺在床上，鬼已經出去了。

en: Then he returned from the region of Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
zh: 耶穌從推羅境內出去，經過西頓，回到低加波利地區的加利利海。

en: And they brought to him a man who was deaf and had a speech impediment, and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
zh: 有人帶著一個又聾又啞的人到他那裡，求耶穌按手在他身上。

en: And taking him aside from the crowd privately, he put his fingers into his ears, and after spitting touched his tongue.
zh: 耶穌把他從人群中帶到一邊，用指頭探他的耳朵，吐唾沫抹他的舌頭，

en: And looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, "Ephphatha," that is, "Be opened."
zh: 然後望著天，長長地歎了一口氣，對他說：“以法大！”意思是“開了吧”。

en: And his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
zh: 那人的耳朵就開了，舌頭也鬆了，說話也準確了。

en: And Jesus charged them to tell no one. But the more he charged them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
zh: 耶穌囑咐他們不要告訴人。但他越是囑咐，他們卻越發傳揚。

en: And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, "He has done all things well. He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak."
zh: 眾人非常驚訝說：“他所作的一切事都好極了；他竟然使聾子聽見，又使啞巴說話。”
